title: PowerShell File Management
description: Learn to create, read, write, move, rename, remove, and verify files and folders in PowerShell.

tasks.prompt: Create a new folder named 'backup' in the current directory.
tasks.explanation: New-Item creates files, directories, and other provider items.

tasks.prompt: Delete the file 'temp.txt' from the current directory.
tasks.hint: Use Remove-Item with the file name.
tasks.explanation: Remove-Item deletes files, folders, and registry keys.

tasks.hint: Use Rename-Item with the old and new names.
tasks.explanation: Rename-Item changes the name of an existing file or folder.

tasks.hint: Use Move-Item with source and destination paths.
tasks.explanation: Move-Item relocates items to a new directory or renames them across paths.

tasks.hint: Use Test-Path with the target path.
tasks.explanation: Test-Path returns True or False depending on whether a path exists.

tasks.prompt: Read and display the contents of 'readme.txt'.
tasks.hint: Use Get-Content with the file name.
tasks.explanation: Get-Content reads text from a file line by line or as a single string.

tasks.prompt: Write the text 'Initial entry' to 'log.txt', overwriting any existing content.
tasks.explanation: Set-Content replaces the entire contents of a file.

tasks.prompt: Append the line 'Second entry' to the end of 'log.txt'.
tasks.explanation: Add-Content appends new text without removing existing file content.
